response.justification: Sarah Smith is a seasoned full-stack and backend web developer with strong distributed systems instincts, but the role demands deep, senior-level C++ expertise as its core requirement. Her C++ exposure is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project; her career since has been almost entirely in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. She has no demonstrated experience with industrial automation, IEC 62443 or SDL security practices, MQTT, OPC UA, or the embedded/on-premises platform domain the role targets, making her profile a poor fit despite her general engineering quality.
strengths: Proven high-performance, low-latency distributed systems work at Sociomantic (millions of HTTP requests/second, async I/O, fibers)
Event-driven and micro-services architecture experience directly applicable to message-broker-based designs
Broad polyglot background (DLang, Node.js, TypeScript, Java, some C/C++) shows adaptability and systems-level thinking
concerns: C++ depth is negligible — only ~6 months at National Instruments a decade ago and a thesis project; nowhere near the 8–12 years of senior C++ the role requires
No industrial automation, IoT, or OT-security background; IEC 62443, Modbus, OPC UA, and SDL practices are entirely absent from the profile
Career trajectory is firmly in web and cloud full-stack development, not embedded or on-premises platform engineering
